mission.title: nats: Ability to reset consumer start sequence
mission.description: Ability to reset consumer start sequence. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current nats deployment
steps.description:
Verify your nats version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
helm list -n nats 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working nats installation.

steps.title: Review nats configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant nats configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl get configmap -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=nats
```
## Feature Request

#### Use Case:

The motivation comes from event sourcing-style use cases, but can apply to general _sourcing_ of a stream into some materialized form.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to reset consumer start sequence
steps.description:
Currently we don't offer durable ordered consumption of a stream. We do support ephemeral ordered push consumers, but those can be problematic when using those on WQ/Interest streams as as soon as the server sends the message the "ack" happens immediately, resulting in the message to be removed. Similarly recreating the consumer (through delete+create) may lose interest and remove messages. Durable consumers could be used for guaranteed ordered consumption by using the "pinning" introduced in

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl get events -n nats --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to reset consumer start sequence" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Currently we don't offer durable ordered consumption of a stream. We do support ephemeral ordered push consumers, but those can be problematic when using those on WQ/Interest streams as as soon as the server sends the message the "ack" happens immediately, resulting in the message to be removed. Similarly recreating the consumer (through delete+create) may lose interest and remove messages.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with nats installed or the issue environment reproducible.
